opening.headline: Frontend systems fail slowly — until they don't.
opening.subtext: I build structures that survive growth.
opening.intro:
My focus is long-term clarity:
clear boundaries, predictable state flow, and decisions that remain defensible as teams expand.

caseStudy.title: Refactoring a Growing Frontend Without Stopping Delivery

problem.intro: The system showed classic scaling symptoms:
bullets: UI tightly coupled with business logic
problem.closing: Shipping became slower. Debugging became harder.

bullets: Measured before and after each change
approach.closing: Refactor as a series of controlled improvements — not a heroic rewrite.

bullets: Increased confidence when shipping new features
result.closing: Good architecture increases team velocity over time.

stateTree.title: State Is a Responsibility, Not a Tool
stateTree.intro: The wrong state decision compounds quietly.

reasoning.Local State: If only one component cares, keep it local.
reasoning.Lifted State: If siblings depend on it, lift it intentionally.
reasoning.Context or Store: If it represents domain-level truth, centralize it.
reasoning.Global Store: Use when multiple independent areas rely on shared data — not as a default.

stateTree.closingLine:
Global state is powerful.
Overuse is expensive.

teamScaling.title: Scaling Frontend Teams Without Losing Clarity

sections.title: Code Reviews
sections.body:
Code reviews should improve architecture, not just formatting.
I look for boundary violations, accidental coupling, and hidden complexity.

sections.title: Abstraction
sections.body:
Abstraction is earned through repetition.
If it hasn't repeated, it hasn't proven itself.

sections.title: Folder Structure
sections.body:
Structure should mirror domain logic — not technical layers.
If a new engineer can't predict where something lives, the structure failed.

sections.title: Documentation
sections.body: Short, clear reasoning scales better than long documentation nobody reads.

lessons.title: Experience Is Accumulated Mistakes
items: I underestimated how fast complexity compounds.
Now I optimize for clarity first.

askMe: When do you refactor vs rewrite?
How do you prevent architecture decay?
How do you decide state boundaries?
How do you scale frontend teams?
